last_excerpt: raised, always noticed when trying to sleep in a long-sleeve shirt. 2. Keys with jagged teeth pressed into a palm, not hurting but never quite comfortable. 3. The sound of distant sirens heard through double-paned glass, muffled into a long, rising whine. 4. Gaps between blinds, slanted lines of light, a stripe of brightness across the carpet. 5. The faint tang after biting aluminum foil. 6. Coldness of tile under bare feet, then the surprise when it warms, but only in the one spot. 7. The word “echo” echoing in the head, softer each time, until it’s just the shape of silence. 8. A single pencil mark on a wall at knee height—nobody knows where it came from. 9. How the air feels different right next to a window, even when it’s closed. --- A piece of lint adrift, almost weightless, circling the edge where the table meets the floor.
last_concepts: the specific weight of a quarter vs a nickel
the warm side of a pillow
the weight of someone else's keys
the sound of a basketball on asphalt vs concrete
the exact moment you realize you're sunburned
